title: Coryphadenia monantha
taxonomy: Coryphadenia monantha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, which includes a diverse group of plants commonly known as acanthus or thorny flowers. This plant is native to Madagascar and is a member of the genus Coryphadenia. The classification of Coryphadenia monantha reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. The family Acanthaceae is composed of approximately 2,455 species, spread across 320 genera. The classification of Coryphadenia monantha helps to understand its relationships with other plants and its place within the broader botanical community.
